data.title: Couche-Tard CEO: We Would Like Full Engagement Versus Just Engagement On Antitrust
data.description:
Alimentation Couche-Tard

  • CEO: I DON’T BELIEVE WE HAVE A STAGNANT BUSINESS IN NORTH AMERICA, THE EARNINGS RESULTS WE HAVE PUT OUT IN RECENT QUARTERS COMPARED TO SEVEN & I ARE VERY DIFFERENT

  • CHAIRMAN: WE ARE STILL OPEN TO TALKING TO THE ITO FAMILY

  • CEO: WE WOULD LIKE FULL ENGAGEMENT VERSUS JUST ENGAGEMENT ON ANTITRUST

  • CEO: WE HAVE ENJOYED THE DIALOGUE ON ANTITRUST, IT'S BEEN CONSTRUCTIVE BUT IS TAKING TOO LONG

  • CEO: THE JANUARY RESTATEMENT OF OUR OFFER IN YEN IS VERY SIMILAR TO OUR U.S. DOLLAR OFFER

  • CEO: THE U.S. ANTI-MONOPOLY PROCESS DOES NOT TAKE TWO YEARS

  • CEO: THE PROCESS COULD TAKE 12-24 MONTHS

  • CHAIRMAN: IN MEETINGS WITH INVESTORS WE SAID WE DON’T KNOW EXACTLY WHAT OUR PLAN FOR SEVEN & I’S SUPERSTORES BUSINESS WOULD BE

  • CEO: WE CAN’T HAVE AN INFORMED DECISION ON THE SUPERSTORES AT THIS STAGE WITHOUT DUE DILIGENCE

  • CHAIRMAN: WE DO NOT INTEND TO CALL OFF OUR BID IF THE ANTITRUST PROCESS IS EXTENDED

  • CHAIRMAN: THE FRUSTRATION WE FEEL ABOUT THE DEAL IS JUST ABOUT THE TIME

data.title: Momentum funds, once star performers, are now in a free fall
data.description:
Momentum Funds, once delivering more than double the returns compared to Nifty 50 Index Fund, are now among the worst performers in this leg of the market downturn, with nearly all of them seeing a steep decline of more than 20 percent over three and six-month period.

Analysts are attributing this underperformance to the nature of momentum investing, which thrives in bullish cycles when stocks are rallying. Momentum funds typically invest in stocks that are sensitive to market cycles.

Nearly 20 momentum funds have suffered substantial losses, including Bandhan Nifty 500 Momentum 50 Index, Motilal Oswal Nifty 500 Momentum 50 ETF, Nippon India Nifty 500 Momentum 50 Index, and Motilal Oswal Nifty 500 Momentum 50, which have plunged over 27 percent in the last three months.

Tata Nifty MidCap 150 Momentum 50 Index Fund, Edelweiss Nifty MidCap 150 Momentum 50 Index, and Kotak Nifty MidCap 150 Momentum 50 Index have fallen over 24 percent. Other momentum funds recorded losses ranging from 13-23 percent.

Funds of this category typically chase stocks with strong recent price momentum, but once markets correct, they often struggle. The performance closely mirrors broader market trends, and a potential recovery -- possibly in the next quarter or the second half of the year -- could help restore investor confidence.

Independent analyst Deepak Jasani said momentum investing thrives in a bullish phase, particularly when broader blue-chip stocks across the market rally.

Stock selection in these funds is more technical than fundamental, and as midcap indices and individual stocks weaken, fund managers are at a risk of losses. Initially, one may wait for a rebound, but they may also be forced to exit at a loss and enter new positions.

In a falling market, momentum is short-lived, making it difficult to find suitable stocks, said experts.

Nirav Karkera, Head of Research at Fisdom said most momentum funds have inadvertently invested in stocks that elevated their aggregate beta, making them highly sensitive to market cycles. While such portfolios typically outperform during cyclical upswings, their long-only nature limits downside protection, making them vulnerable in bearish phases.

Experts said passively managed momentum funds take time to adjust to market dynamics. However, actively managed momentum funds are rebalanced more frequently. In the recent fall, actively managed momentum funds have fallen less compared to passively managed ones.

Rupesh Bhansali, Head of Mutual Funds at GEPL Capital, cautions that momentum funds inherently carry high risk. Investors should consider these funds only with a long-term horizon of more than a year, while those with a shorter time-frame should avoid them.

Experts further say that investors keen on low costs and systematic momentum investing with no fund manager risks should stick to passively managed index funds with five years view. In the long term, these funds can reward investors in a structurally bull market. Corrective phases can be used to accumulate units of these schemes, experts added.Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by experts on Moneycontrol are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: Bank of America highlights some harrowing numbers on the size of US government spending
data.description:
Republicans are still riding high from an electoral victory but they will soon have to face some real facts: The US has a deficit problem.

At the moment, they appear to be living in a fantasyland where they can aggressively cut taxes while DOGE does a dog-and-pony show around saving money. In reality, there are only four places to cut US spending that will make any difference: the military, medicare, medicaid and social security.

Those are electorally sacred and leave them with a tougher choice: Roll back tax cuts or run a huge deficit.

The WSJ today reports that all the tax cuts are still on the table and that the price tag is enormous. There appears to be some kind of effort at creative accounting and I suspect that's the direction they will go in.

If not, Bank of America highlights how dependent the US has been on growing jobs via deficits near 7% of GDP:

This isn't going to be easy and I don't think it will be pretty. Again, I think the path of least resistance (as usual) is running up the deficit but the Republican majority is slim and there may be some true deficit hawks out there.

NTPC Green Arm Declares Remaining 50MW Capacity of Shajapur Solar Project Commercially Operational
data.description:
NTPC Green Energy has declared commercially operational the remaining 50 MW capacity of the 105 MW Shajapur Solar Project (Unit-1) in India of its subsidiary NTPC Renewable Energy, according to a Wednesday filing to the Indian bourses.

The initial capacity of 55 MW was declared commercially operational on Nov. 29, 2024.

data.title: Better Looking U.S. Inflation Data No Green Light for Fed Cuts — Market Talk
data.description: The latest U.S. inflation numbers look better, but the all-clear for the Federal Reserve to restart interest-rate cuts hasn't been sounded, unless there is a shock to U.S. growth, says Cedric Chehab, chief economist at BMI. A big part of the disinflation in the U.S. has been driven by declines in goods prices over recent months, he notes. Large Trump administration tariffs on goods could engineer a jump in prices very soon, which means goods will again add to inflation, he says. This makes it difficult to forecast inflation over the near term and for the Fed to provide proper guidance, he adds. (james.glynn@wsj.com; @JamesGlynnWSJ)

data.title: Metals trade mixed; zinc at over 7-week high on Nyrstar's output cut
data.description:
London metals traded mixed on Thursday as a cooler-than-expected U.S. inflation print bolstered rate cut expectations, while zinc surged to a more than seven-week high on Nyrstar cutting production in Australia.

U.S. consumer price index in February increased less than expected, but the improvement is likely temporary against the backdrop of aggressive tariffs on imports.

U.S. President Donald Trump lashed out at Canada on Tuesday over its retaliation for his prior tariffs by doubling the import duty on Canadian steel and aluminum to 50%, while keeping rates for all other countries at 25% and rescinding prior exemptions, exclusions and quotas.

Three-month zinc on the London Metal Exchange (LME) gained 0.8% to $2,946.5 a metric ton as of 0234 GMT.

It scaled a seven-week peak earlier in the session after Nyrstar on Wednesday announced 25% production cuts at its Hobart zinc operations in Australia from April, sending prices higher.

The company owned by commodity trader Trafigura said its Australian assets continue to face significant financial challenges due to worsening conditions in raw material markets, negative treatment charges and increased costs.

The zinc smelter in Hobart, which Nyrstar bills as one of the world's largest, has a production capacity of about 260,000 metric tons a year, the company said in an email, without providing the latest output figures.

LME copper gained 0.5% to $9,805 a ton, LME aluminium remained flat at $2,702.5 a ton, lead lost 0.1% to $2,079, tin eased 0.1% to $33,380 and nickel was unchanged at $16,635.

SHFE copper climbed 1.25% to 78,760 yuan ($10,892.44) a metric ton, SHFE aluminium rose 0.1% to 20,960 yuan a ton, zinc added 0.9% to 24,155 yuan, tin advanced 0.6% to 265,500 yuan, lead jumped 0.8% to 17,580 yuan and nickel soared 0.8% to 133,590 yuan.

($1 = 7.2307 Chinese yuan renminbi)

data.title: Gold rises as tariff uncertainty, cooler inflation data lend support
data.description:
Key points:
  • $3000/oz is the next logical target for gold, analyst says
  • U.S. CPI increased less than expected last month
  • U.S. PPI data due at 1230 GMT

Gold prices edged higher on Thursday as uncertainty over tariffs persisted, driving safe-haven demand, while a cooler-than-expected U.S. inflation print also supported bullion by strengthening expectations of rate cuts.

Spot gold was up 0.4% at $2,943.66 an ounce, as of 0300 GMT, while U.S. gold futures firmed 0.2% to$2,951.90.

"I think $3,000 is the next logical target, likely reached sometime over the next several months," said Marex analyst Edward Meir. "CPI data was encouraging but I suspect that the tariff increase has yet to be picked up in the inflation data."

Data showed that the U.S. consumer price index increased less than expected last month, but the improvement is likely temporary against the backdrop of aggressive tariffs on imports that are expected to raise the cost of most goods in the months ahead.

Lower inflation leaves more room for the U.S. Federal Reserve to cut interest rates, and non-yielding gold thrives in a low-interest rate setting.

Trump early this month triggered a trade war, increasing the tariffs on goods from China to 20% and imposing a new 25% duty on Canadian and Mexican imports.

He later dialled back and provided a one month exemption for any goods that meet the rules of origin under the U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement on trade.

Trump also reversed course on Tuesday afternoon on a pledge to double tariffs on steel and aluminum from Canada to 50%, hours after announcing the higher tariffs.

Trump's tariffs are widely expected to stoke inflation and economic uncertainty, and have prompted safe-haven gold to reach a record high of $2,956.15 on February 24.

Investors now await U.S. Producer Price Index (PPI) data due later in the day to gain further insights into the Fed's monetary policy.

Spot silver rose 0.1% to $33.26 an ounce, platinum added 0.4% to $987.90, and palladium gained 0.9% to $957.60.

data.title: Crude Palm Oil Prices Should Remain Supported by Tight Supply — Market Talk
data.description: Crude palm oil prices should remain supported by Malaysia's supply tightness in the near term, say UOB Kay Hian analysts in a note. Malaysia's palm oil stocks fell 4.3% on month in February, according to the Malaysian Palm Oil Board. However, factors such as the seasonal ramp-up in CPO production could begin exerting downward pressure on prices in the coming months, they say. UOB Kay Hian maintains a market weight rating on Malaysia's plantation sector. The analysts have a buy rating on Hap Seng Plantations and SD Guthrie, as they continue to like these companies for their upstream business, favorable production trends, and high dividend yields.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Wednesday at 11 PM ET
data.description:
Stocks Fluctuate on Muted Inflation Data, Loud Trade Fight

The S&P 500 and Nasdaq ended higher after investors weighed the latest trade skirmish with Canada and a solid inflation report. The Dow industrials edged down.

U.S. Federal Deficit Exceeds $1 Trillion Over 5 Months

Data disclosed by the Treasury Department showed an adjusted gap between federal spending and revenue of $1.063 trillion for October through February.

Trump set to nominate Michelle Bowman as Fed's top bank supervisor

President Donald Trump is set to nominate Federal Reserve Governor Michelle Bowman to be the central bank's top bank supervisor, a White House official confirmed to MarketWatch.

Consumer Angst Is Striking All Income Levels

Signs of weakness are showing up in spending on everything from basics to luxuries.

The Stock Market Is Finally Rallying. It May Be a Head Fake.

A tame inflation report is welcome news, but markets remain worried about President Donald Trump's tariffs and trade war.

Europe, Canada Hit Back at U.S. Steel Tariffs

The EU is targeting a range of American goods, including whiskey and motorcycles, with 50% tariffs, taking aim at politically sensitive products.

Inflation Cooled to 2.8% in February, Lower Than Expected

Inflation eased last month, but the data may offer less comfort to businesses, consumers and the Fed than it otherwise would as looming tariffs may keep prices rising.

Bank of Canada Cuts Policy Rate to 2.75% as Economy Faces 'New Crisis'

The central bank must balance the risk of tariff-fueled higher inflation and the damage to growth from U.S. trade policy, Gov. Tiff Macklem says.

WTO's Trade Barometer Picks Up, Pointing to Resilient Flows

The World Trade Organization's trade barometer rose, but it warned increased purchases by those seeking to get ahead of threatened tariffs might exaggerate the health of the global trading system.

Wind and Solar Overtake Coal Power for First Time in U.S.

The two renewable energy sources accounted for 17% of the country's power mix while coal fell to a low of 15%. Natural gas remains by far the largest source of electricity, accounting for 43%.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Wednesday at 11 PM ET
data.description:
Intel Names Lip-Bu Tan as Chief Executive Officer

The former CEO of Cadence Design Systems, steps in as the semiconductor manufacturer has lost market share.

Adobe Stock Slides Despite Earnings Beat. The CFO Is Confident About AI.

Adobe posted first-quarter adjusted earnings of $5.08 a share, beating estimates of $4.97 a share.

LVMH Taps Arnault's Son to Head Loro Piana in Leadership Reshuffle

The fashion role is expected to bolster Frédéric Arnault's credentials as a candidate to one day lead the luxury-goods giant.

Puma Will Cut 500 Positions Globally as Part of a Cost-Cutting Plan

Job reductions will be implemented at the group's headquarters in Germany and in regional offices. The company has a total workforce of 22,500.

D-Wave Claims 'Quantum Supremacy,' Beating Traditional Computers

D-Wave is the latest quantum computing company to say it has achieved "quantum supremacy," better known as the ability to solve a problem with a quantum computer that can't be done with a traditional computer.

Niantic to Sell Pokemon GO, Other Games to Saudi-Backed Group in $3.5 Billion Deal

Niantic is also selling "Pikmin Bloom," and "Monster Hunter Now," Niantic's most recent game.

Sunnova Prepares Restructuring Talks That Could Include Bankruptcy Filing

Sunnova Energy, one of the country's largest residential solar companies, is preparing to engage with creditors to explore options including a potential bankruptcy filing over its roughly $8.5 billion in debt, according to people familiar with the matter.

Wonder Acquires Tastemade for Its Latest Quest: to Make a 'Mealtime Super App'

Wonder CEO Marc Lore hopes the company will one day offer AI-powered autonomous food ordering for all three meals of the day.

Whiskey Is a Target of Europe's Retaliatory Tariffs. Brown-Forman Stock Slides.

The European Union plans to establish countermeasures in April that apply to products ranging from boats to bourbon.

Target Beefs Up Grocery Supply Chain as Consumer Spending Shifts

The retailer is bringing its supply chain for fresh and frozen foods in house to better forecast demand and plan inventory.

data.title: Kingston Resources Says Strategic Process for Papua-New Guinea Gold Mine Ongoing, Shares Rise 4%
data.description:
Kingston Resources said the strategic process in relation to its Misima gold project in Papua New Guinea is ongoing, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

It appointed Argonaut PCF as the exclusive advisor to lead and manage a formal strategic process for the project in November 2024.

The mining firm said there is currently no certainty of outcome for the strategic process.

its shares rose 4% in recent trading on Thursday.

data.title: Market Chatter: Japan Post Offloads 592 Billion Yen Worth of Shares in Banking Unit
data.description:
Japan Post Holdings completed the sale of 592 billion ($4 billion) worth of Japan Post Bank shares, the largest stock sale in the country since 2023, Bloomberg News reported, citing data from the finance ministry.

The company sold 410 million shares at 1,444 yen each, reflecting a 2% discount from the previous closing price.

This move aligns with Japan Post Holdings' commitment to reduce its stake in the bank to below 50% by the end of the fiscal year through March, the report said.

For Japan Post Bank, the sale facilitates greater operational flexibility by reducing the need for government approvals, according to the report.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Bermaz Auto Could Face Increasing Competition — Market Talk
data.description: Bermaz Auto will likely face a challenging outlook in fiscal 2025 due to increasing competition from Chinese-made vehicles, says TA Securities analyst Angeline Chin in a note. These vehicles are competitively priced and boast advanced connectivity features, she writes. The company holds the electric-vehicle distributorship for Deepal, which has a new model slated to be launched in Malaysia by 4Q, she notes. That should help diversify and expand its product portfolio for new customer segments, she adds. However, Chin doesn't expect significant contributions from Deepal, given the many EV options available, which makes the segment highly competitive. TA Securities has a sell rating on the stock and a target price of MYR0.94. Shares are at MYR1.02. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: Lens Technology Plans to List in Hong Kong; Shares Down 7%
data.description:
Lens Technology plans to list its shares on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange filing on Thursday.

The company's board of directors approved the proposal, which is still subject to regulatory approvals, the lens product manufacturer said.

The company's shares dropped less than 7% in recent trade.

data.title: Levi & Korsinsky Announces the Filing of a Securities Class Action on Behalf of e.l.f. Beauty, Inc.(ELF) Shareholders
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / March 12, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your e.l.f. Beauty, Inc. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/e-l-f-beauty-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=135647&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against e.l.f. Beauty, Inc. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between November 1, 2023 and November 19, 2024.

CASE DETAILS: The filed complaint alleges that defendants made false statements and/or concealed that: (i) contrary to its representations to investors, the Company was experiencing rising inventory levels as a consequence of flagging sales; (ii) Elf falsely attributed the rising inventory levels to, among other things, changes in its sourcing practices; (iii) to maintain investor confidence, Elf reported inflated revenue, profits, and inventory over several quarters; (iv) the Company's business and/or financial prospects were overstated; (v) all of the foregoing, once revealed, would likely have a material negative impact on the Company; and (vi) as a result, the Company's public statements were materially false and misleading at all relevant times.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in e.l.f. Beauty stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/e-l-f-beauty-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=135647&wire=1 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. There is no cost or obligation to participate.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

data.title: Market Chatter: Korean Steelmakers Hit by US Tariffs, Weigh Countermeasures
data.description:
South Korean steelmakers, including Posco and Hyundai Steel , face new challenges as the US imposed 25% tariffs on steel and aluminum imports on Wednesday, Yonhap News said in a same-day report.

Companies plan to cooperate with the government and develop high-end products to maintain market share, with Posco considering a US plant.

Seoul's request for exemptions was unsuccessful, adding pressure on Korean firms amid growing competition with US steel producers, the report said.

Shares of Posco rose more than 1%, while those of Hyundai Steel fell nearly 1% in recent trade.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Novonix Receives Approval to Acquire Tennessee Land for Production Plant; Shares Up 10%
data.description:
Novonix received unanimous approval from each of the City of Chattanooga and Hamilton County in Tennessee to acquire a 182-acre parcel of land for $4.6 million for its second mass production plant, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

The plant is expected to reach its full production capacity of 31,500 tonnes per year by the end of 2028, the filing added.

The company's shares rose 10% in recent Thursday trade.

The 885.1 million-yuan transaction was approved by the China Securities Regulatory Commission, according to a Thursday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

data.title: Bank of Korea says wary of FX volatility on trade war risks
data.description:
South Korea's central bank said on Thursday that U.S. President Donald Trump's escalating trade war could drag on longer than expected and increase the risk of capital outflows while also raising volatility in the dollar-won market.

"Considering that the value of the won is greatly affected by fluctuations in the value of the yuan as well as the dollar, there is a possibility that the volatility of the dollar-won exchange rate will increase significantly," the Bank of Korea (BOK) said in a monetary policy report, which predicted that a trade war between the world's two largest economies could intensify.

The report said the BOK will continue to "closely monitor foreign investment and the currency movement" as it remains wary of foreign investors pulling money out of Asia's fourth-largest economy.

The won has gained 1.6% against the dollar so far this year, but dropped 14% last year, making it one of the worst-performing currencies in Asia.

The BOK on Feb. 25 cut interest rates by 25 basis points and significantly lowered its GDP forecasts, the third monetary easing since it started cutting rates from a 15-year high in October.

South Korea is grappling with the economic impact of U.S. President Donald Trump's ongoing tariff war, which is likely to undermine corporate profits.

Domestic political unrest, following the brief declaration of martial law by impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol in December, also continues to dampen consumer sentiment.
